source.title: Attractor dynamics in the hippocampal representation of the local environment

confidence_rationale: Cleanest published demonstration of bistability between coexisting hippocampal representations of the same physical place. Direct empirical anchor for hard dual-trace switching in MECH-269. Caveat: bistability required familiarisation, and the trigger appears to be input-distance not anchor V_s.
failure_signatures: Bistability emerged only after pre-exposure created the two attractors. Anchors not yet consolidated will behave as graded representations, not discrete bistable basins -- the substrate must handle both regimes.
Switching was driven by current input crossing the basin boundary, not by anchor-internal staleness. If MECH-272 routing relies on V_s to trigger switching, switching will lag the input change.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Hippocampal CA1 ensembles switch abruptly and simultaneously between two attractor states (square-mode vs circle-mode) as environmental shape is morphed incrementally, after pre-exposure has established each shape as a familiar context.
mapping.ree_translation: Direct biological evidence for two coexisting attractor representations of the same physical place, with near-step switching between them under input morphing -- the central architectural claim of MECH-269 dual-trace anchor preservation. MECH-272 routing maps onto the basin-boundary crossing.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Bistability required prior familiarisation. The substrate cannot assume dual-trace anchors arise immediately from a single experience. The switching is input-distance driven, not V_s driven.
mapping.source_context: CA1 single-unit recordings in rats over 7-day morphing protocol (square <-> circle, intermediate octagonal shapes).

confidence_components.notes: Very high source quality; high mapping fidelity for the qualitative substrate claim. Rodent-only and geometric-morphing-only constrain transfer.
